Concept-stage strategy report

MyDockAI Internal Master Report

A decision-ready internal report for shaping MyDockAI as an AI agent operating layer for sales teams in Southeast Asia.

Recommended beachhead: IT service / MSP sales Concept stage: no traction claims Offline editable HTML
Core positioning
Agent layer

Not a generic chatbot, not a CRM replacement, and not a workflow engine brand.

Architecture stance
CRM first

CRM remains source of truth; MyDockAI stores data for RAG and orchestrates actions.

Go-to-market wedge
Pilot MVP

Sell a controlled pilotable MVP, not a full SaaS foundation on day one.

Executive Strategy Console

Decision dashboard from the sample direction

The sample console used investor-style metrics and evidence cards. In this report they are treated as working assumptions to validate, not external market facts or proven ROI.

Executive summary

MyDockAI provides an AI agent operating layer purpose-built for sales teams to increase pipeline handling capacity, improve sales productivity, and enhance customer experience across every channel.

$9.3B TAM placeholder to validate against SEA sales-agent platform scope
21% Rep productivity lift target for pilot validation
3.2x ROI ambition over a three-year payback model
Low-Med Managed risk target after guardrails and phased rollout

Key takeaways

  • Large opportunity driven by AI adoption, seller productivity needs, and fragmented customer channels.
  • Clear differentiation requires domain-specific agents, CRM action connectivity, and data trust.
  • ROI potential depends on reducing repeated work, improving response speed, and keeping CRM state clean.
  • Execution focus: integrations, RAG quality, governance, and change management.

Decision log

What is already locked

This section separates firm decisions from recommendations. It prevents the report from overclaiming product maturity or hiding unresolved risk.

Locked Use MyDockAI only

Remove legacy engine naming from final positioning. Use MyDockAI channels, AI control channels, or agent control plane.

Locked Cloud SaaS only

Private cloud and self-hosting stay out of scope except as future enterprise considerations.

Locked Full data stored for RAG

MyDockAI stores full conversations, leads, and knowledge-base data while CRM remains operational source of truth.

Recommendation Use a guardrail proxy

Do not expose autonomous customer actions directly without policy checks, tool scopes, logs, and rollback controls.

Recommendation Beachhead IT service / MSP

Best balance of repeatable sales workflows, CRM literacy, integration tolerance, and lower sensitivity than funeral or iGaming.

Recommendation Scroll-first report UX

Best fit for internal reading, editing, and clean export. Deck mode adds complexity without improving the core artifact.

Market signals

Why this opportunity exists in SEA

The strongest near-term opportunity is not "AI" by itself. It is the gap between customer conversations that happen everywhere and sales workflows that still depend on manual CRM discipline.

Digital behavior

Messaging is a natural work surface

DataReportal reports that WhatsApp is the favorite social platform globally among surveyed users aged 16+, and SEA is structurally social and mobile heavy. This supports a sales-control experience that starts in messaging, not only in a CRM dashboard. Source

Enterprise adoption

AI demand is high, but execution is uneven

Google, Temasek, and Bain frame SEA as moving from digital decade to AI reality. The opportunity is to translate interest into usable workflows and measurable commercial outcomes. Source

SME readiness

SMEs need applied workflows, not model choice

OECD analysis of SME AI adoption emphasizes practical business benefits and barriers. MyDockAI should package AI inside sales tasks, policy controls, and CRM writeback rather than selling raw model access. Source

Market problem statement

Sales teams lose opportunities because response speed, follow-up quality, lead qualification, and multilingual handling depend on individual human discipline across disconnected channels. The missing layer is not another inbox; it is a governed agent operating layer that can read context, take action, and keep the CRM updated.

Competitive landscape

Where MyDockAI can be different

Most visible competitors cluster around omnichannel inboxes, CRM-native agents, customer support agents, or marketing automation. The proposed wedge is salesperson control plus CRM source-of-truth plus configurable autonomy.

Category Examples Strength Gap MyDockAI can exploit
Omnichannel conversation platforms respond.io, SleekFlow Strong channel aggregation, WhatsApp/social workflows, team inboxes, CRM sync. Position away from inbox management toward salesperson command and CRM action control.
CRM-native AI agents Salesforce Agentforce, HubSpot Breeze Deep CRM data access, enterprise credibility, native workflow automation. CRM-agnostic control plane for teams that do not want to live inside one CRM suite.
Support automation suites Zendesk AI Agents, Intercom Fin Customer-service automation, handoff, analytics, resolution workflows. Sales-first workflow language: lead summary, follow-up, qualification, next action, deal updates.
Workflow automation tools Low-code automation engines Broad connectors, rapid workflow composition, AI workflow nodes. MyDockAI should hide workflow plumbing and sell business outcomes, not workflow building.

Recommended differentiation

Sell "AI sales butler through your existing channels" for salespeople, and "governed agent operating layer that keeps CRM clean" for leadership.

Positioning risk

If MyDockAI looks like a lighter respond.io/SleekFlow clone, the product will be compared on inbox features. The strongest angle is not inbox UI; it is action orchestration and CRM writeback from messaging control channels.

Beachhead recommendation

Start with IT service / MSP sales

The report compares all four candidate markets, then recommends IT service / MSP sales as the first beachhead because it balances sales urgency, workflow repeatability, integration readiness, and manageable reputational risk.

Market Why attractive Main risk Fit score Recommendation
IT service / MSP sales Clear pre-sales repetition, proposal support, technical FAQ, lead qualification, CRM literacy. Needs strong knowledge accuracy and integration discipline. 8.7 Beachhead pilot.
General SME sales teams Large market, broad pain, easy narrative. Too diffuse; workflows vary widely and sales cycles may become consultative. 7.2 Use as horizontal expansion after repeatable playbook.
Funeral industry sales teams High urgency, 24/7 need, lead response matters. Emotionally sensitive; wrong tone creates high brand damage. 6.8 Second pilot after guardrail confidence.
iGaming / VIP support High volume, multilingual, segmented customers, VIP workflows. Regulatory, reputational, and compliance complexity. 6.1 Defer unless a committed design partner exists.

Leadership decision

Approve IT service / MSP as the first validation segment. Keep the report honest: this is a recommended wedge, not proof of product-market fit.

Sales framing

"Your salespeople can ask for lead summaries, next actions, follow-up drafts, and CRM updates from the channels they already use."

Recommended next build

Build first

Pilotable AI control plane for IT/MSP sales workflows

Automate lead summary, next-best action, response drafting, CRM writeback, RAG retrieval, and policy-blocked autonomy from MyDockAI control channels.

Why now

  • High impact for SEA sales teams.
  • Clear differentiation versus broad inbox platforms.
  • Feasible in Phase 1 with workflow orchestration and CRM-first state.

Unresolved decisions

Expose agent directly vs guarded proxyHigh impact

Decide whether customer-facing automation must always pass through policy orchestration.

Connector strategyMedium impact

Choose workflow-orchestration-first, native connectors, generic API/webhooks, or hybrid.

Pricing model refinementMedium impact

Validate current subscription + setup + AI-credit baseline against real cost and support load.

Phase 1
Foundation
Phase 2
Scale

Phase 1 foundation

  • Core workflow builder for sales use cases.
  • Pre-built connectors for CRM, email, and messaging.
  • Reliability, permissions, and audit logs.

Phase 2 scale

  • Advanced automation patterns.
  • Template library and reusable industry playbooks.
  • Team collaboration and observability.

Phase 3 expand

  • Enterprise controls and stronger admin policies.
  • API and developer platform.
  • Industry solutions and partner ecosystem.

Product thesis

MyDockAI as the sales agent operating layer

The product should behave like an AI butler for sales teams: context-aware, action-capable, policy-governed, and CRM-synchronized.

For salespeople

  • Ask for a lead summary from WhatsApp or Telegram-style control channels.
  • Request reply drafts, next actions, and follow-up reminders.
  • Trigger CRM updates without opening the CRM.
  • Escalate uncertain or sensitive cases to a manager.

For sales managers

  • Track response speed, follow-up coverage, hot leads, and agent outcomes.
  • Set policy boundaries for autonomous actions.
  • Review risk events, overrides, and CRM writeback quality.
  • Use weekly reports to improve sales workflows.

For admins

  • Connect CRM, channels, knowledge sources, and model-provider routing.
  • Configure retention, tenant isolation, roles, and action policies.
  • Manage knowledge-base ingestion and RAG quality checks.
  • Monitor cost, credits, and provider usage.

Important contradiction to manage

MyDockAI stores full conversation, lead, and knowledge-base data for RAG, but CRM remains the operational source of truth. The product must explicitly define what is authoritative: CRM owns current state and deal record; MyDockAI owns AI memory, summaries, policies, usage, and action history.

Architecture

Recommended operating model

Use a CRM-first source-of-truth model with a MyDockAI agent control plane in front of workflow orchestration. This gives salespeople a simple command surface while keeping CRM data authoritative.

Decision queue

AI autonomy policy levelsHigh

Decide autonomy thresholds for respond, assist, escalate, and CRM writeback.

Owner: Head of Product

Guardrail and compliance scopeHigh

Confirm required guardrails, data policies, regional rules, and escalation logging.

Owner: Risk / Product

Source-of-truth finalizationMedium

Validate CRM as single operational source and define stale-data reconciliation.

Owner: RevOps / Engineering

Human-in-the-loop modelMedium

Define review requirements, exception handling, and manager override flows.

Owner: Sales Enablement

Rollout phasing and segmentsLow

Approve pilot segment, expansion plan, and change-management path.

Owner: Head of Sales

Workflow orchestration layer

Playbooks
  • Use-case flows
  • Task templates
  • SOPs
Agent network
  • Specialist agents
  • Skills and tools
  • Handoffs
Observability
  • Traces and logs
  • Quality scores
  • Cost and latency

CRM source of truth detail

  • Accounts, contacts, opportunities, activities.
  • Products, tickets, documents, and relationships.
  • Permissions and data-integrity controls.
  • Bidirectional sync with audit trail and conflict handling.
  • System integrations: ERP/billing, CPQ/pricing, support tools.

Salesperson control channels

  • My tasks: inbox, next-best actions, follow-ups.
  • Conversation workspace: thread, context, agent insights.
  • Account 360: timeline, insights, related records.
  • Recommendations: next best action, playbooks, content.
  • Reporting and insights: performance, quality, pipeline impact.
  • Settings: autonomy, notifications, guardrails.

Recommended connector path

Use workflow orchestration first for connector breadth, then add native connectors only when priority CRMs justify reliability and support investment.

Provider strategy

Provider-agnostic from day one with customer-selectable model providers. MyDockAI owns provider accounts and bills AI model usage via credits.

Failure mode

Full omnichannel from day one increases integration, policy, and support load. The MVP must reduce behavior scope even if channel coverage is broad.

Proposed architecture and flow PNGs

Leadership-ready visual blueprint

These generated diagrams convert the architecture and operating model into shareable visuals for internal sales, leadership, and product alignment. The detailed text sections remain editable for wording changes; the PNGs provide a fast visual walkthrough.

Proposed architecture

Use this diagram to explain the recommended CRM-first model: customer channels feed the MyDockAI agent control plane, workflow orchestration executes actions, CRM remains the source of truth, and salespeople operate through lightweight command channels.

Proposed flow design

Use this diagram to align on the operating flow: every customer message is normalized, matched to a lead, enriched with context, routed through the AI butler and guardrail check, then either written back automatically or reviewed by a salesperson before customer follow-up.

Diagram caveat

The PNGs are proposed visuals, not final system diagrams. Treat labels, branches, and channel examples as editable decision prompts until engineering confirms API contracts, permission boundaries, data retention, and rollout sequence.

Safety and governance

Layered guardrails, not one approval switch

Because autonomy is the default unless blocked by policy, governance must be action-aware. Gartner warns that one-size-fits-all agent governance can cause enterprise AI agent failure. Source

Risk tier Example action Default behavior Controls
Read-only Summarize lead, retrieve conversation, explain status. Autonomous. Tenant isolation, access permissions, audit log.
Low-risk internal Create note, tag lead, create follow-up task. Autonomous unless policy blocks. Tool scopes, reversible writeback, rate limits.
Customer-facing Reply to lead, send follow-up, answer product question. Autonomous for approved intents and knowledge-backed answers. RAG grounding, tone policy, hallucination checks, fallback to salesperson.
High-impact commercial Pricing, proposal, contract, refund, deal-stage movement. Blocked or approval-required by default. Manager approval, policy exceptions, rollback, incident review.

Prompt injection and tool abuse

OWASP's LLM Top 10 frames prompt injection, sensitive information disclosure, and excessive agency as core LLM application risks. MyDockAI must treat channel messages and CRM fields as untrusted input. Source

Brand and reputation

Wrong tone in urgent or emotional sectors can be worse than no automation. Funeral and VIP/iGaming use cases require stronger tone, escalation, and compliance defaults.

Agent design principle

Use scoped tools, explicit instructions, evaluation, human escalation, and observability. OpenAI's agent guide emphasizes agents that combine instructions, tools, guardrails, and handoffs. Source

Build strategy

Recommended path: pilotable MVP with feasibility gates

The report should not recommend a full SaaS foundation as the first build. The right first move is a sellable pilot MVP backed by technical spikes for channel reliability, CRM sync, RAG, and guardrails.

Phase 0

2-4 weeks

Prototype control-channel experience, CRM writeback, RAG retrieval, and policy blocking. Validate one IT/MSP demo scenario end to end.

Phase 1

6-8 weeks

Pilotable MVP across selected omnichannel inputs, CRM source of truth, lead summaries, reply generation, and action logs.

Phase 2

8-12 weeks

Harden tenant isolation, provider routing, credits, admin policies, role permissions, reporting, and onboarding workflow.

Phase 3

Scale

Package vertical playbooks, expand CRM connectors, add manager dashboards, and refine pricing based on pilot usage.

First acceptance criteria

  • Lead summary generated from multi-channel context.
  • CRM writeback succeeds and is auditable.
  • Policy engine blocks at least one configured high-risk action.
  • Salesperson can operate without logging into CRM.

Do not skip

Run failure-mode testing before any direct customer automation: stale CRM data, wrong customer identity, prompt injection, repeated replies, policy bypass, and provider outage.

Sales enablement

How to explain MyDockAI internally and externally

Sales should avoid selling "AI magic." The stronger narrative is controlled capacity: faster response, better follow-up, cleaner CRM, and more time for closing.

One-line pitch

MyDockAI gives sales teams an AI control layer that handles repetitive lead work across channels while keeping the CRM updated.

Customer pain

Leads come through WhatsApp, web chat, email, CRM, and social. Salespeople respond manually, forget follow-ups, and leave CRM data stale.

Product promise

Ask MyDockAI for summaries, next actions, follow-up drafts, CRM updates, and lead reports from the channels your team already uses.

Objection handling

Is this just ChatGPT?
No. ChatGPT is a model interface. MyDockAI is a sales operating layer that connects channels, CRM, workflow actions, knowledge, policies, and reporting.
Will it replace salespeople?
No. The pitch is capacity and consistency. AI handles repetitive work; salespeople focus on judgment, relationship, and closing.
Is it safe to let AI reply?
Only within configured policies. High-risk actions can be blocked, approval-gated, or escalated.

Demo storyline

  1. A lead arrives from WhatsApp and website chat.
  2. MyDockAI resolves the customer and summarizes intent.
  3. Salesperson asks for next best action from a control channel.
  4. AI drafts the response and updates CRM after action.
  5. Manager sees hot leads and follow-up coverage.

Pricing and commercial model

Use current pricing as baseline, not final truth

The deck baseline is useful for initial sales conversations, but the real economics depend on model usage, support load, connector complexity, and channel pass-through costs.

Starter baseline

1-5 users

10,000 AI credits, USD9.90 per user, setup fee from USD1000.

Pro baseline

1-20 users

25,000 AI credits, USD24.90 per user, setup fee from USD1000.

Enterprise

Custom

Custom users, credits, onboarding, policy controls, connector needs, and service-level expectations.

Pricing lever Recommendation Reason
Subscription Keep per-user tiers for simplicity. Easy for SME buyers to understand.
Credits Meter AI model usage only. Matches confirmed direction and avoids confusing workflow/channel costs with AI consumption.
Setup fee Keep setup fee for workflow analysis and deployment. Protects margin because implementation effort is real.
Channel costs Treat as pass-through or separate add-on. WhatsApp and other messaging costs may change externally and should not be hidden inside AI credits.

Verification

How success should be measured

Concept-stage reports often over-index on ROI claims. This one should propose measurable pilot signals instead.

62% Resolution rate without human target
2m 48s First response time target
81% Agent assist rate target
$1.18 Cost per interaction working target

Pilot business metrics

Response speedHigh
Follow-up coverageHigh
Lead capacityMed
CRM hygieneMed

Reliability and trust metrics

  • Lead identity match accuracy.
  • CRM writeback success and rollback rate.
  • Human override rate by action tier.
  • Hallucination or unsupported-answer rate.
  • Policy-block false positive and false negative rate.
  • Cost per resolved or assisted lead action.

Risk overview from control-plane sample

Risk Status Mitigation direction
Compliance risk High Policy guardrails, audit trail, action scopes.
Data privacy risk High Tenant isolation, access control, retention rules.
Adoption risk Medium Control channels, manager reports, phased rollout.
Model performance risk Medium Provider routing, evaluation, fallback escalation.
68%
complete

HTML artifact recommendation

Scroll-first internal report wins

The final artifact itself should be scroll-first, not reveal.js slides. It remains presentation-friendly through sticky navigation, concise sections, and filters.

Format Pros Cons Decision
Scroll report Best for dense internal reading, citations, editing, export, and maintenance. Less theatrical for live pitching. Recommended.
Deck-style slides Better for live pitch rhythm and simple presenter flow. Poorer for detailed feasibility, citations, and editable long-form content. Use separate sales deck later.
Hybrid Can support both reading and presenting. Higher complexity and export risk for little first-version gain. Defer.
